medications.drug_name: betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% ointment (potent topical steroid) — short course
medications.indication_rationale: Short course (~5 days) to the blistering areas to rapidly suppress the severe inflammatory (id) reaction.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply thinly to blistering areas twice daily for ~5 days (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Treats the underlying dermatophyte; apply to the whole foot daily for ~4 weeks.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to the whole foot daily for ~4 weeks (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: The intact blister roof protects the raw skin from infection — do not pop them.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the feet exposed to air (open-toed shoes/barefoot at home), dry the feet well, wash socks hot and spray boots with antifungal, and don't pop the blisters.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes the sweaty occlusive environment and lets the blisters dry safely.

patient_education_points.point: These blisters are a strong immune reaction to athlete's-foot fungus, set off by sweaty boots — not an allergy or burn. A short course of strong steroid ointment settles the blistering, and the antifungal cream (whole foot, 4 weeks) treats the cause.
Don't pop the blisters — the roof protects the skin. Keep your feet aired and dry, wash socks hot and treat your boots.
If the blister fluid turns to thick yellow pus, the redness becomes hot and spreads up the ankle, or you develop a fever — go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution of the blistering and ongoing antifungal treatment; consider oral antifungal for nails/refractory disease.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Purulent blisters, ascending hot redness, or fever — emergency care.

must_include_items: Recognise vesiculobullous tinea pedis (from concurrent classic tinea) vs pompholyx/pustulosis/impetigo
Short potent topical steroid for the blistering + whole-foot antifungal (treat the cause)
Airing/drying/footwear measures; do NOT pop the blisters
acceptable_alternatives: Alternative topical antifungal; oral antifungal for nails/refractory disease
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires treating the fungal cause (whole-foot antifungal) + a short steroid for the blistering + not popping the blisters, with a secondary-infection safety-net.
